caption: Table C-2 City of Union City 2017 LHMP Incorporation
extracted_text: Table C-2 City of Union City 2017 LHMP Incorporation Planning Mechanism 2017 LHMP Was Incorporated/Implemented In. Details: How was it incorporated? General Plan Integrates Hazard Mitigation through consideration of hazards most likely to impact Union City. This includes Earthquake, flooding, fires etc. Municipal Code Title 2 Chapter 2.28 talks of the Emergency Organization, Disaster Council, and considers as appropriate the LHMP C.3 Community Profile The community profile for the City of Union City is detailed in the following sections. Figure C-1 displays a City map and the location of Union City within the Tri-City Planning Area.

caption: Table C-19) and the City’s overall Vulnerability ranking.  Vulnerability is measured in general, qualitative
extracted_text: Table C-19) and the City’s overall Vulnerability ranking. Vulnerability is measured in general, qualitative terms and is a summary of the potential impact based on past occurrences, spatial extent, and damage and casualty potential. It is categorized into the following classifications: ➢ Extremely Low—The occurrence and potential cost of damage to life and property is very minimal to nonexistent. ➢ Low—Minimal potential impact. The occurrence and potential cost of damage to life and property is minimal. ➢ Medium—Moderate potential impact. This ranking carries a moderate threat level to the general population and/or built environment. Here the potential damage is more isolated and less costly than a more widespread disaster. ➢ High—Widespread potential impact. This ranking carries a high threat to the general population and/or built environment. The potential for damage is widespread. Hazards in this category may have occurred in the past. ➢ Extremely High—Very widespread with catastrophic impact. This is followed by the following sections: ➢ Hazard Profile and Problem Description – A hazard profile is included for each hazard. This includes

caption: Table C-60 summarizes Table C-59 above and shows City of Union City loss estimates and improved
extracted_text: Table C-60 summarizes Table C-59 above and shows City of Union City loss estimates and improved values at risk by FEMA 1% and 0.2% annual chance flood zones. According to Table C-59 and Table C-60, the City of Union City has 386 parcels and $693 million of structure and contents values or values in the 1% annual chance flood zone, and 255 improved parcels and $230 million of structure and contents values in the 0.2% annual chance flood zone. These values can be refined a step further. Applying the 20 percent damage factor as previously described in Section 4.3.11 of the Base Plan, there is a 1% chance in any given year of a flood event causing $139 million in damage and a 0.2% chance in any given year of a flood event causing $46 million in damage in the City of Union City. The loss ratio of 1.09% and 0.36% indicates that flood losses for 1% and 0.2% annual chance flooding would be relatively minor (respectively) and the City would be able recover relatively quickly.
